notes.content: The Disability Studies Major embraces a methodology that examines disability as a social, cultural and aesthetic construct, manifest in and shaped by artistic practices of representation and embodiment, that influences the perception of whose lives are valuable to society, and that serves as the basis for a range of policies, norms, and practices. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for the major, students must complete the following coursework by the end of spring before transfer: Disability Studies 1, One course in Social Theory, One course in Race, Identity & Society, One course in Humanities & Ethics, and One course in Data Analysis. All courses must be completed for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's selection process, visit https://uei.ucla.edu/academic-programs/disability-studies and https:/ / admission.ucla.edu. In addition, the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.
notes.contentHtml:
The Disability Studies Major embraces a methodology that examines disability as a social, cultural and aesthetic construct, manifest in and shaped by artistic practices of representation and embodiment, that influences the perception of whose lives are valuable to society, and that serves as the basis for a range of policies, norms, and practices.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for the major, students must complete the following coursework by the end of spring before transfer:

  • Disability Studies 1,
  • One course in Social Theory,
  • One course in Race, Identity & Society,
  • One course in Humanities & Ethics, and
  • One course in Data Analysis.

All courses must be completed for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's selection process, visit https://uei.ucla.edu/academic-programs/disability-studies and https://admission.ucla.edu.


In addition, the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the disability studies major prep: A score of 3 or higher on the AP Statistics exam can be used to satisfy the data analysis course.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the disability studies major prep:

  • A score of 3 or higher on the AP Statistics exam can be used to satisfy the data analysis course.
